metadata.summary: On the evening of March 12, 2024, a physical assault occurred outside the Shree Ram Grocery Store in Shivaji Nagar, Pune. The victim, Arjun Deshmukh, a local vegetable vendor, was attacked by an unidentified man who struck him repeatedly with a wooden plank, causing a fractured left radius and multiple contusions. Arjun was rushed to Ruby Hall Clinic, where his injuries were documented. CCTV footage from the grocery store’s entrance captured the assault, showing a man in a dark hoodie and blue jeans fleeing the scene. Three witnesses — Mrs. Meena Kulkarni (neighbor), Rajesh Patil (shop assistant), and Vikram Joshi (passerby) — provided statements to the Shivaji Nagar Police Station. All three identified the suspect as Rohan Deshmukh, Arjun’s estranged cousin, who had a documented history of verbal altercations with Arjun over a disputed family land parcel. Police recovered a blood-stained wooden plank near the alley behind the store, matching the injury pattern. However, Rohan claims he was at his sister’s wedding in Satara that evening and produced a wedding invitation and photo as alibi evidence. The case appears straightforward, but inconsistencies in witness timing and a minor discrepancy in the CCTV timestamp raise questions about the reliability of the identification.

hidden_ground_truth.true_motive: Revenge for Arjun reporting Ramesh’s illegal alcohol distillation operation to the excise department two weeks prior
true_timeline: 2024-03-12 18:15: Ramesh Kulkarni arrives at Shivaji Nagar on a rented scooter from Pimpri
2024-03-12 18:30: Ramesh waits near Shree Ram Grocery Store, observing Arjun closing up
2024-03-12 18:37: Ramesh strikes Arjun with a wooden plank from behind, then flees on foot toward the alley
2024-03-12 18:42: Ramesh discards the plank behind the store and takes a rickshaw to the Pune railway station
2024-03-12 19:15: Ramesh boards a train to Satara, arriving at 21:30 — just in time to be seen at the wedding reception
2024-03-12 20:00: Ramesh uses a borrowed phone to send a photo of himself at the wedding to his sister, who later provides it as alibi evidence
forged_evidence: Wedding photo presented by Ramesh Kulkarni — digitally altered to include his face in a group photo taken at 20:15, while he was still en route to Satara

injected_contradictions.description: Mrs. Meena Kulkarni claims she saw the attacker’s face clearly at 18:35, but her statement says she was looking out her window at 18:30 — the CCTV timestamp shows the attack began at 18:37, and her window faces the opposite direction
Rajesh Patil says the attacker was wearing a blue hoodie, but the CCTV footage shows a dark grey hoodie — this was later 'corrected' in the police report to match Rohan’s known clothing

Vikram Joshi says he saw the attacker run toward the railway station, but his initial statement to the constable said 'toward the bus stand' — this was changed after a police officer suggested the railway station was more logical

contradictions.claim_a: Mrs. Meena Kulkarni claims she saw the attacker’s face clearly at 18:30 while looking out her window.
contradictions.claim_b: CCTV footage confirms the assault began at 18:37, and Mrs. Kulkarni’s window faces away from the alley where the attack occurred.

contradictions.claim_a: Rajesh Patil states the attacker wore a bright blue hoodie.
contradictions.claim_b: CCTV footage clearly shows the attacker wearing a dark grey hoodie with no visible blue.

contradictions.claim_a: The wooden plank recovered has blood matching Arjun Deshmukh’s DNA and fingerprints of Rohan Deshmukh.
contradictions.claim_b: Rohan’s fingerprints were found on the plank only because he had visited the store to buy vegetables two days earlier — the blood was planted after the fact using a swab from the hospital.

nodes.label: Assault at Shree Ram Grocery Store
